Pregnancy Support | Shasta County CA
Toggle Menu
Pregnancy Support
Pregnancy Support helps pregnant women get early and regular prenatal care. The program connects women with a doctor while they are applying for Medi-Cal.
Call (530) 225-5850 or (800) 300-5122 to get started.
Prenatal Care Guidance
The Prenatal Care Guidance Program helps pregnant women with limited income obtain early and regular prenatal care.
Visit the Prenatal Guidance Program page of the Shasta HHSA website for more information.
Presumptive Eligibility
The Presumptive Eligibility program is for low-income pregnant women who need immediate, temporary coverage for prenatal care and have not yet applied for Medi-Cal.
Visit the California Department of Healthcare Services website for more information
.
Medi-Cal Access Program (MCAP)
For women who don't have insurance to cover pregnancy and are not receiving no-cost Medi-Cal or Medicare Part A and Part B or have other health insurance plan that doesn't cover maternity services or with a maternity-only deductible or copayment greater than $500.
Family PACT
If your insurance does not cover birth control, pregnancy testing or family planning, you may be qualified for Family PACT. It provides no-cost family planning services to women and men, including teens, living on a low-income.
Visit the Family PACT website for more information
.
